text: Lesson 21
zh: 第21课

text: Daniel Mendoza
zh: 丹尼尔·门多萨

text: How many unsuccessful attempts did Mendoza make before becoming Champion of all England?
zh: 门多萨在成为全英格兰冠军之前，经历了多少次失败的尝试？

text: Boxing matches were very popular in England two hundred years ago.
zh: 两百年前，拳击比赛在英国非常受欢迎。

text: In those days, boxers fought with bare fists for prize money.
zh: 那时，拳击手们赤手空拳地搏斗，以争夺奖金。

text: Because of this, they were known as 'prizefighters'.
zh: 正因如此，他们被称为“职业拳击手”。

text: However, boxing was very crude,
zh: 然而，那时的拳击运动十分粗野，

text: for there were no rules and a prizefighter could be seriously injured or even killed during a match.
zh: 因为没有任何规则，拳击手在比赛中可能会受重伤，甚至丧命。

text: One of the most colourful figures in boxing history was Daniel Mendoza, who was born in 1764.
zh: 拳击史上最富传奇色彩的人物之一，便是1764年出生的丹尼尔·门多萨。

text: The use of gloves was not introduced until 1860, when the Marquis of Queensberry drew up the first set of rules.
zh: 拳击手套直到1860年才被引入，当时昆斯伯里侯爵制定了第一套拳击规则。

text: Though he was technically a prizefighter,
zh: 尽管从严格意义上说他是一名职业拳击手，

text: Mendoza did much to change crude prizefighting into a sport, for he brought science to the game.
zh: 门多萨却为将粗野的拳击变成一项运动做出了巨大贡献，因为他为这项运动带来了科学的方法。

text: In his day, Mendoza enjoyed tremendous popularity.
zh: 在他所处的时代，门多萨享有极高的声誉。

text: He was adored by rich and poor alike.
zh: 无论贫富，人人都对他敬爱有加。

text: Mendoza rose to fame swiftly after a boxing match when he was only fourteen years old.
zh: 门多萨年仅十四岁时的一场拳击赛后便迅速成名。

text: This attracted the attention of Richard Humphries who was then the most eminent boxer in England.
zh: 这场比赛引起了理查德·汉弗莱斯的注意，他当时是英格兰最杰出的拳击手。

text: He offered to train Mendoza and his young pupil was quick to learn.
zh: 他主动提出训练门多萨，而这位年轻的弟子学得很快。

text: In fact, Mendoza soon became so successful that Humphries turned against him.
zh: 事实上，门多萨很快就变得极为出色，以至于汉弗莱斯反而与他反目成仇。

text: The two men quarrelled bitterly and it was clear that the argument could only be settled by a fight.
zh: 两人争执激烈，显然只有通过一场比赛才能了断这场纷争。

text: A match was held at Stilton, where both men fought for an hour.
zh: 比赛在斯蒂尔顿举行，两人激战了一个小时。

text: The public bet a great deal of money on Mendoza, but he was defeated.
zh: 公众在门多萨身上下了大量赌注，但他却输了。

text: Mendoza met Humphries in the ring on a later occasion and he lost for a second time.
zh: 后来门多萨再次与汉弗莱斯在拳击场上交锋，结果又一次落败。

text: It was not until his third match in 1790 that he finally beat Humphries and became Champion of England.
zh: 直到1790年的第三场比赛，他才终于击败汉弗莱斯，成为英格兰冠军。

text: Meanwhile, he founded a highly successful Academy and even Lord Byron became one of his pupils.
zh: 与此同时，他创办了一所非常成功的拳击学院，就连拜伦勋爵也成了他的学生。

text: He earned enormous sums of money and was paid as much as ￡100 for a single appearance.
zh: 他赚得盆满钵满，一次登场表演的报酬竟高达一百英镑。

text: Despite this, he was so extravagant that he was always in debt.
zh: 尽管如此，他挥霍无度，始终债台高筑。

text: After he was defeated by a boxer called Gentleman Jackson, he was quickly forgotten.
zh: 在被一位名叫“绅士”杰克逊的拳击手击败后，他很快便被人遗忘。

text: He was sent to prison for failing to pay his debts and died in poverty in 1836.
zh: 他因无力偿还债务而被送进监狱，于1836年在贫困中去世。
